instruction: open terminal windows in the bottom of the android studio

location.label: Terminal tool window button in bottom-left toolbar
location.name: Terminal tool window button in bottom-left toolbar
location.reasoning: The current tabs visible (Problems, File, Project Errors) do not include Terminal. The three icons on the left strip of the bottom panel (z10, z12, z11) are tool window buttons. The bottom-most icon (z11) is the most likely Terminal button based on typical Android Studio layout, where Terminal appears as a small command-prompt rectangle near the bottom of the tool window strip.

history.target_visible_element: Terminal tab in the bottom toolbar of Android Studio
history.reasoning: Stage 1 crop: selecting the Android Studio application window which spans roughly from x=360 to x=2640 and y=460 to y=1490. This includes the bottom toolbar where the Terminal tab is located. The Terminal button can be seen among the bottom panel options (Version Control, Profiler, Logcat, etc.). Next round will zoom into the bottom toolbar area to precisely target the Terminal tab/button.

history.target_visible_element: Terminal button in Android Studio bottom toolbar
history.reasoning: The task is to open the Terminal window in the bottom of Android Studio. The bottom toolbar icons (z13, z18, z22 at y~1045-1070) are the tool window toggle buttons, which include Terminal, Build, Logcat, etc. I need to crop closer to these bottom toolbar icons to identify the Terminal button specifically. Keeping the leftmost cluster with some context above (the 'Problems/File/Project Errors' tabs area) for orientation.

history.target_visible_element: Bottom panel left toolbar icons in Android Studio
history.reasoning: The current crop shows the Android Studio bottom panel with tabs (Problems/File/Project Errors) and left toolbar icons (grid, lightbulb, small icon). The three OCR candidates (z0, z1, z2) are at y≈1435 in the same row as the lightbulb, which is the row of tool window buttons. The terminal button is typically located in this left toolbar. Cropping to include both rows of icons (the tab bar row and the tool window button row) plus the small icon row below, along with some surrounding context, to identify which icon is the terminal button.
